title: asteriscus pygmaeus
intro: Asteriscus pygmaeus, also known as Dwarf Calendula or Pygmy Chrysanthemum, is a flowering plant species native to North Africa and the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and exhibiting antiseptic properties. Asteriscus pygmaeus is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical applications for skin and wound care.
